steps.title: Use the contrast in the sentence
steps.content: The sentence says works from the post-revolutionary period make up a certain proportion of the Mexican literary canon, but nineteenth-century writers should be considered just as integral. The word "but" signals that the current proportion is too large compared with the attention those earlier writers deserve. The blank needs a word meaning disproportionately large.

steps.title: Eliminate mismatched meanings
steps.content:
• Choice A — unspecified: This means not clearly stated or defined, but the sentence is not saying the proportion is unknown. It is judging the size of that proportion.
  • Choice B — overlooked: This means ignored or neglected. That is the opposite of the idea that post-revolutionary works take up too much space in the canon.
  • Choice C — erratic: This means unpredictable or irregular. The sentence is not about inconsistency; it is about whether the share of the canon is too large.
Choice D, "outsized," means larger than is appropriate or proportional, which matches the contrast with nineteenth-century writers. The answer is D.
